input:
Premise: Suppose there is a closed system of 5 variables, A, B, C, D and E. All the statistical relations among these 5 variables are as follows: A correlates with B. A correlates with C. A correlates with D. A correlates with E. B correlates with C. B correlates with D. B correlates with E. C correlates with D. C correlates with E. D correlates with E. However, C and E are independent given A and B. C and E are independent given A, B and D. D and E are independent given A and B. D and E are independent given A, B and C.
Hypothesis: There exists at least one confounder (i.e., common cause) of C and D.
